mnemonics: No Single Queen Rules All Lands (NoSQL): No Single Query Language, Relaxed Schema, Aggregates, Large-scale
keyPoints: NoSQL systems are designed for horizontal scaling and can handle large volumes of data.
They use a variety of data models including key-value, document, column-family, and graph.
NoSQL databases often sacrifice strict ACID properties for better performance and availability, following the BASE principle.
commonMistakes: Assuming NoSQL means no schema at all, when it often means a flexible or dynamic schema.
Thinking NoSQL is a replacement for all relational databases, rather than a tool for specific use cases like big data or real-time web apps.
Believing that joins are not possible in NoSQL systems, when they are just handled differently (e.g., through application code or data duplication).
